title: No error handling for session.command() response in verification scripts
close_reason: Added error checking for session.command() response in all 5 verification scripts. Now throws immediately with the actual error instead of timing out with a confusing message.

title: eslint-disable comment leaks into published dist output
description:
**File:** src/plugins/hello-world.ts → dist/plugins/hello-world.js (line 1)

The `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` comment on line 8 of hello-world.ts is preserved in the compiled dist/plugins/hello-world.js output. This is a development-only annotation that has no meaning in the published package and adds noise for consumers reading the compiled output.

**Options to fix:**
1. Configure TypeScript to strip comments: add `"removeComments": true` to tsconfig.json compilerOptions
2. Restructure the code to avoid the eslint-disable (e.g., prefix unused param with underscore: `_config`)

Option 2 is preferred as it also addresses the root cause — the `config` parameter triggers the no-unused-vars rule because it's genuinely unused. Using `_config` is the TypeScript convention for intentionally unused parameters.
close_reason: Removed eslint-disable comments by using _config convention and eslint rule configuration

title: hello-world.ts: Two eslint-disable comments suggest rule configuration issue
description:
**File:** src/plugins/hello-world.ts, lines 3 and 8

Two eslint-disable comments are used:
- Line 3: `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-empty-object-type` for the empty `HelloWorldPluginConfig` interface
- Line 8: `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` for the `config` parameter

Both are legitimate patterns in this codebase:
1. Empty interfaces reserved for future use are a valid design pattern
2. Optional parameters that exist for API compatibility are intentional

Rather than disabling rules inline, consider:
- For `no-empty-object-type`: Add a rule override in eslint.config.js to allow empty interfaces (or use `Record<string, never>` as the type)
- For `no-unused-vars`: Rename to `_config` which TypeScript/ESLint conventionally treats as intentionally unused

This would eliminate the inline suppressions and keep the source cleaner.
close_reason: Replaced inline eslint-disable with _config convention and allowInterfaces/argsIgnorePattern rule config

title: prepublishOnly uses bun instead of npm-compatible commands
description:
**File:** package.json, line 19

The `prepublishOnly` script uses `bun run clean && bun run build`:
```json
"prepublishOnly": "bun run clean && bun run build"
```

This script runs during `npm publish`, which means it will fail for anyone publishing with npm if they don't have bun installed. The `prepublishOnly` hook is triggered by npm's publish lifecycle, so it should use `npm run` (or just reference the commands directly) for maximum compatibility:

```json
"prepublishOnly": "npm run clean && npm run build"
```

Both `npm run` and `bun run` will work when bun is the runtime, but only `npm run` works when npm is the runtime. Since this is a published package, the publish lifecycle should be runtime-agnostic.

Alternatively, use the commands directly:
```json
"prepublishOnly": "rm -rf dist/ && tsc"
```
close_reason: Changed prepublishOnly to use npm run instead of bun run

title: package.json: Missing author, keywords, bugs, and homepage fields
description:
**File:** package.json

Several optional but recommended npm package metadata fields are missing:

- `author`: Should be "Brandon Dennis" (matches LICENSE copyright holder)
- `keywords`: Helps discoverability on npm (e.g., `["opencode", "plugin", "workflow", "automation"]`)
- `bugs`: Link to GitHub issues (`https://github.com/toady00/open-mardis-gras/issues`)
- `homepage`: Link to repo or docs

These aren't required for functionality but improve the npm listing and help users find/report issues.
close_reason: Added author, keywords, bugs, and homepage fields to package.json

title: Unsafe (p as { text: string }).text cast instead of using TextPart type guard
description:
In verify-then-prompt.ts line 47, verify-then-sequence.ts line 50, and verify-then-nested.ts line 50, the inline assertion code uses:

```typescript
.some((p) => (p as { text: string }).text.toLowerCase().includes(...))
```

After filtering by `p.type === 'text'`, the part is known to be a TextPart. The code should use the TextPart type from the SDK (already imported in helpers.ts) instead of an ad-hoc cast:

```typescript
.filter((p): p is TextPart => p.type === 'text')
.some((p) => p.text.toLowerCase().includes(...))
```

The helpers.ts file already has this exact pattern in `getTextContent()` (line 49). The inline code should either use the helper or follow the same type-guard pattern. The current cast would silently break if the Part union changes.
close_reason: Added assertContentPresentInRole helper to helpers.ts with proper TextPart type guard. Refactored all 3 scripts to use the helper instead of inline code with unsafe casts.

title: Fix BeadsPlugin: replace session.prompt injection with system.transform
description:
BeadsPlugin uses client.session.prompt() with noReply:true to inject beads context on first message. This creates a user message that races with ThenChainingPlugin's chain registration and triggers extra LLM turns, breaking then-chains.

Fix: Replace the session.prompt injection mechanism with experimental.chat.system.transform hook. This appends beads context to the system prompt on every LLM call, eliminating the race condition entirely. The beads context becomes system instructions rather than a user message.

Key changes:
1. Remove chat.message hook for injection (and the session.prompt call)
2. Add experimental.chat.system.transform hook that appends beads context
3. Run bd prime once per session (or after compaction), cache the output
4. The system transform hook appends cached context to system[] array
5. Keep session.idle bd sync and compaction refresh logic
6. Simplify coordinator usage (no longer need to defer injection during chains since system prompt is always safe)
close_reason: Replaced session.prompt() injection with experimental.chat.system.transform hook. Beads context now appends to system prompt instead of creating user messages, eliminating race conditions with ThenChainingPlugin and preventing extra LLM turns.

title: hello-world.ts: No error handling for client.app.log failure
close_reason: Wrapped client.app.log in try/catch for graceful failure

title: setup.ts cleanup() is fire-and-forget — caller cannot await temp dir removal
close_reason: Fixed: cleanup returns Promise, dist/index.js existence check added, assertContentOrder handles same-message case, stream.return uses valid void cast.

title: executor.ts: Command name may need leading slash stripped for SDK
close_reason: Investigated. The command name is passed as-is to client.session.command(). The readCommandThenChain function already strips the leading / for file lookup. Whether the SDK expects / or not is unclear from types alone, but keeping it consistent with user input (including /) is the safest default. Integration tests will verify the correct behavior.

title: then-chaining.ts: No logging when a new chain starts
description:
In plugins/then-chaining.ts, when a new chain is successfully pushed onto the state manager (lines 125 and 129), there is no log message:

```typescript
// Line 125 - user interruption then fresh chain
stateManager.pushChain(sessionID, entries, command)

// Line 129 - no active chain, start new one
stateManager.pushChain(sessionID, entries, command)
```

The plugin logs when chains are interrupted, when max depth is exceeded, when entries are dispatched, and when chains complete. But the initial chain start is not logged. This makes it harder to debug chain execution because you can't see when a chain was initiated.

Suggested fix: Add a log message like:
```typescript
await logger('info', `Then chain: started for command ${command} with ${entries.length} entries`)
```

Also, for the nested chain case (line 106), a log like:
```typescript
await logger('info', `Then chain: pushed nested chain for command ${command} (depth: ${stateManager.currentDepth(sessionID)})`)
```

Affected file: src/plugins/then-chaining.ts, lines 104-130.
close_reason: Added logging when new chains start and when nested chains are pushed. Log messages include command name, entry count, and depth for nested chains.

title: Spec: Initial Scaffolding for OpenCode Plugin
description:
# Initial Scaffolding Specification

## Overview

Create the foundational project structure for `open-mardi-gras`, a TypeScript-based OpenCode plugin that provides workflow automation features (starting with command chaining/return functionality). This scaffolding establishes the npm package structure, build system, TypeScript configuration, linting, and open source files required for publishing to the npm registry.

**IMPORTANT**: The `.opencode/` directory contains OpenCode's configuration and helper plugins for development purposes ONLY. It is NOT part of this plugin package and should be completely ignored in the published npm package.

**Goal**: Produce a working npm package that exports OpenCode plugins and can be installed via `npm install open-mardi-gras` or referenced in `opencode.json`.

## Requirements

### 1. Package Configuration

**1.1 Root package.json**
- Package name: `open-mardi-gras`
- Initial version: `0.1.0`
- Description: "OpenCode plugin for workflow automation and command chaining"
- Type: `module` (ES modules)
- Main entry: `./dist/index.js`
- Types entry: `./dist/index.d.ts`
- Files to include: `["dist/", "LICENSE", "README.md", "CHANGELOG.md"]` (explicitly excludes `.opencode/`)
- Scripts needed:
  - `build`: Compile TypeScript to `dist/` (`tsc`)
  - `lint`: Run ESLint (`eslint src/`)
  - `lint:fix`: Fix ESLint issues (`eslint src/ --fix`)
  - `clean`: Remove `dist/` directory (`rm -rf dist/`)
  - `prepublishOnly`: Run `clean` and `build` (clean must run first to avoid stale files)
  - `dev` (optional): Watch mode (`tsc --watch`)
- Engines field:
  - `node`: ">=18.0.0" (supports current LTS)

**1.2 Dependencies**
- `@opencode-ai/plugin`: `^1.2.10` (devDependency only - OpenCode guarantees it's available at runtime)
- `typescript`: `^5.x` (devDependency)
- `@types/node`: `^20.x` (devDependency)
- `eslint`: `^9.x` (devDependency)
- `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`: `^8.x` (devDependency)
- `@typescript-eslint/parser`: `^8.x` (devDependency)

### 2. TypeScript Configuration

**2.1 tsconfig.json**
- Target: ES2022
- Module: NodeNext
- ModuleResolution: NodeNext
- Declaration: true (generate .d.ts files)
- DeclarationMap: true
- SourceMap: true
- Strict: true
- OutDir: `./dist`
- RootDir: `./src`
- SkipLibCheck: true
- EsModuleInterop: true
- Exclude: `[".opencode/"]` (don't compile dev helpers)

### 3. ESLint Configuration

**3.1 eslint.config.js**
- Use ESLint flat config format (eslint.config.js)
- Include `@typescript-eslint/recommended` rules
- Include `@typescript-eslint/recommended-type-checked` rules
- Target files: `src/**/*.ts`
- Ignore: `dist/`, `node_modules/`, `.opencode/`
- Include parserOptions with project reference to tsconfig.json for type-aware linting

### 4. Project Structure

```
open-mardi-gras/
├── package.json           # NPM package configuration
├── tsconfig.json          # TypeScript compiler configuration
├── eslint.config.js       # ESLint configuration
├── LICENSE                # MIT license file
├── CHANGELOG.md           # Changelog following common-changelog format
├── README.md              # Existing - enhance with development info
├── .gitignore             # Add dist/, node_modules/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts           # Main entry point - exports HelloWorldPlugin
│   └── plugins/
│       └── hello-world.ts # Validation plugin (hello world)
└── dist/                  # Compiled output (gitignored)
```

**Note**: `.opencode/` directory exists at root but is NOT part of the plugin package.

### 5. Source Code Files

**5.1 src/plugins/hello-world.ts**
- Simple "Hello World" OpenCode plugin to validate wiring
- Implements the Plugin type from `@opencode-ai/plugin`
- Logs a message on initialization
- Accepts optional configuration object (even if unused) to support future configuration needs
- Example structure:
  ```typescript
  import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
  
  export interface HelloWorldPluginConfig {
    // Reserved for future configuration options
  }
  
  export const HelloWorldPlugin = (config?: HelloWorldPluginConfig): Plugin => {
    return async ({ client }) => {
      await client.app.log({
        body: {
          service: "open-mardi-gras",
          level: "info",
          message: "HelloWorldPlugin initialized",
        },
      })
      
      return {
        // Empty hooks object - this plugin just validates wiring
      }
    }
  }
  ```

**5.2 src/index.ts**
- Main entry point for the plugin package
- Export HelloWorldPlugin from `./plugins/hello-world.js`
- Export HelloWorldPluginConfig type for TypeScript users
- Re-export types from `@opencode-ai/plugin` if needed
- Structure:
  ```typescript
  export { HelloWorldPlugin } from './plugins/hello-world.js'
  export type { HelloWorldPluginConfig } from './plugins/hello-world.js'
  export type * from '@opencode-ai/plugin'
  ```

### 6. Open Source Files

**6.1 LICENSE**
- MIT License
- Include copyright notice: "Copyright (c) 2026 Brandon Dennis"
- Use standard MIT license text

**6.2 CHANGELOG.md**
- Follow [Common Changelog](https://common-changelog.org/) format
- Initial version: 0.1.0
- Sections: "0.1.0 - 2026-02-26" with "Initial release" description
- Include link to full git history for details

**6.3 README.md (Enhancements)**
Add sections:
- Installation (npm install)
- Development setup (bun install, build, lint)
- Plugin usage in opencode.json (both string reference and import with config)
  ```typescript
  // Option 1: String reference (simplest)
  plugins: ['open-mardi-gras']
  
  // Option 2: Import with configuration (for advanced use)
  import { HelloWorldPlugin } from 'open-mardi-gras'
  plugins: [HelloWorldPlugin({ /* options */ })]
  ```
- Contributing (brief)
- License reference
- Changelog reference

### 7. Build System

**7.1 Compilation**
- Use `tsc` for building (no bundler needed for now)
- Output to `dist/` directory
- Include .d.ts files for TypeScript consumers
- Include source maps for debugging
- Must NOT compile `.opencode/` directory

**7.2 Watch Mode**
- `dev` script using `tsc --watch`

### 8. Git Configuration

**8.1 .gitignore additions**
```
# Build output
dist/

# Dependencies
node_modules/

# OS
.DS_Store

# Temporary files
*.tmp
*.swp
```

**8.2 Do NOT add to .gitignore**
- `.opencode/` - this directory IS tracked in git (contains dev helpers)

**8.3 npm publish exclusions**
- Use `files` field in package.json: `["dist/", "LICENSE", "README.md", "CHANGELOG.md"]`
- This explicitly excludes `.opencode/` from npm package

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Running `bun install` installs all dependencies
- [ ] Running `bun run build` compiles TypeScript without errors
- [ ] Running `bun run lint` checks code style without critical errors
- [ ] Running `bun run lint:fix` fixes auto-fixable ESLint issues
- [ ] Running `bun run clean` removes the `dist/` directory
- [ ] Running `bun run dev` starts TypeScript watch mode
- [ ] `dist/` directory contains compiled `.js` and `.d.ts` files
- [ ] `dist/` contains `.js.map` source map files
- [ ] `dist/` contains `.d.ts.map` declaration map files
- [ ] `dist/index.js` is the main entry point
- [ ] `dist/index.d.ts` provides TypeScript definitions
- [ ] `tsconfig.json` is valid (running `tsc --noEmit` succeeds)
- [ ] `eslint.config.js` is valid (running `eslint --print-config src/index.ts` succeeds)
- [ ] LICENSE file exists with standard MIT text and copyright "Brandon Dennis"
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md exists following common-changelog format with date 2026-02-26
- [ ] README includes development instructions
- [ ] README documents both string and import plugin usage patterns
- [ ] Package can be linked locally with `bun link` for testing
- [ ] Running `npm pack` creates a valid tarball without errors
- [ ] Running `npm pack --dry-run` shows only expected files (dist/, LICENSE, README.md, CHANGELOG.md)
- [ ] Running `npm publish --dry-run` succeeds without errors
- [ ] `files` field in package.json correctly limits published files
- [ ] `.opencode/` directory is NOT included in the published npm package
- [ ] `.opencode/` directory IS tracked in git (not gitignored)
- [ ] `git check-ignore dist/` returns dist/ (is ignored)
- [ ] `git check-ignore node_modules/` returns node_modules/ (is ignored)
- [ ] @opencode-ai/plugin is NOT in dependencies or peerDependencies (devDependency only)
- [ ] HelloWorldPlugin can be imported and used in an OpenCode configuration
- [ ] Installing the package via `npm install` and importing it works without errors
- [ ] Plugin accepts optional configuration object (even if unused in v0.1.0)

## Edge Cases

### Bun vs npm Compatibility
- Use `bun` for development (as per OpenCode)
- Ensure package works with standard `npm`/`node` consumers
- Lock file: Use `bun.lock` (Bun's format)
- Windows compatibility: `clean` script uses `rm -rf` (Unix-only); Windows support deferred to future release

### ESM-Only Package
- Do not generate CommonJS output
- Set `"type": "module"` in package.json
- Use `.js` extensions in imports (TypeScript handles this)
- Ensure `"module": "NodeNext"` in tsconfig.json

### Dependency Handling
- `@opencode-ai/plugin` is a devDependency only
- OpenCode guarantees runtime availability
- No peerDependencies complexity needed
- Type checking works during development

### Build Artifacts
- `dist/` is gitignored but included in npm package via `files` field
- Source maps included for debugging
- Declaration maps for IDE go-to-definition

### .opencode/ Directory Handling
- **In Git**: `.opencode/` IS tracked (helpers shared across dev environments)
- **In Build**: TypeScript excludes `.opencode/` from compilation
- **In npm**: `.opencode/` is NOT published (via `files` field in package.json)
- **In ESLint**: Ignored from linting (development helpers, not package code)

### Build Failures
- If TypeScript compilation fails, output must show file/line/column of errors
- Build process should exit with non-zero status code on failure
- `clean` script must complete successfully before `build` runs (order matters in prepublishOnly)

### Stale Build Artifacts
- `clean` script must remove entire `dist/` directory before rebuilding
- Stale files in dist/ must not be included in published package

### npm Package Verification
- `npm pack --dry-run` must show only expected files
- Published package must not include .opencode/, src/, or config files
- Package must be installable via `npm install` without errors

### Runtime Errors
- Plugin must fail gracefully if OpenCode client is unavailable
- Missing @opencode-ai/plugin at runtime should produce clear error message

## Dependencies

This epic has no dependencies. It is the foundational work.

## Child Issues

After this scaffolding is complete, the following child issues will be created:

1. **"Implement Return Plugin"** (separate epic)
   - Depends on: This scaffolding epic
   - Implements the command chaining feature described in README
   - Adds return.ts to src/plugins/
   - Updates index.ts exports

## Next Steps After Scaffolding

1. Verify scaffolding acceptance criteria all pass
2. Close this epic
3. Create child epic: "Implement Return Plugin" (depends on this epic)
4. Test locally with `bun link`
5. Create npm account and publish v0.1.0
6. Update documentation with real npm install instructions

## References

- OpenCode Plugin Documentation: https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/
- Bun Documentation: https://bun.sh/docs
- TypeScript ESM Guide: https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/esm-node.html
- npm Package Publishing: https://docs.npmjs.com/packages-and-modules/contributing-packages-to-the-registry
- npm files field: https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v10/configuring-npm/package-json#files
- Common Changelog: https://common-changelog.org/
close_reason: All scaffolding complete. Package builds, lints, and packs cleanly. All 9 implementation beads and 7 review findings resolved. Ready for v0.1.0 publish.

title: Duplicated inline user-message assertion logic in 3 verification scripts
description:
verify-then-prompt.ts (lines 43-55), verify-then-sequence.ts (lines 46-58), and verify-then-nested.ts (lines 46-58) all contain nearly identical inline code that:
1. Filters messages by user role
2. Checks if any user message's text parts contain a specific substring
3. Prints pass/fail output

This pattern should be extracted into a shared helper in helpers.ts, e.g.:

```typescript
export function assertUserMessageContains(
  messages: MessageWithParts[],
  substring: string,
): boolean
```

This would reduce duplication, ensure consistent formatting, and make it easier to fix bugs (like the `(p as { text: string }).text` cast) in one place.
close_reason: Added assertContentPresentInRole helper to helpers.ts with proper TextPart type guard. Refactored all 3 scripts to use the helper instead of inline code with unsafe casts.

title: waitForIdle timeout only fires on event arrival — hangs if SSE stream stalls
close_reason: Fixed: waitForIdle now uses Promise.race with a setTimeout-based timeout promise, so hangs are impossible even if the SSE stream stalls or the server crashes.

title: state.ts: hasActiveChain returns true for exhausted-but-unpopped frames
close_reason: hasActiveChain now checks if any frame in the stack has remaining entries (currentIndex < entries.length), preventing false positives for exhausted-but-unpopped frames.

title: executor.ts: Recursive unwinding in processNext should be iterative
close_reason: Converted processNext from recursive to iterative using a while loop. Frame unwinding and error recovery both use continue instead of recursive calls.

title: stream.return(undefined as never) is a fragile cast for closing SSE generator
description:
In test/integration/helpers.ts line 43, the SSE stream is closed with:

```typescript
await stream.return(undefined as never)
```

The `as never` cast is needed because the AsyncGenerator's return type parameter doesn't match `undefined`. This works but is fragile — if the SDK changes the generator's TReturn type, the cast silently suppresses the type error.

A cleaner approach would be to use an AbortController (if the SDK supports it) or to accept the type mismatch explicitly:

```typescript
// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
await stream.return(undefined as any)
```

Or better yet, restructure waitForIdle to use the AbortController pattern suggested in the timeout fix (omg-8p1), which would eliminate the need for manual stream cleanup.
close_reason: Fixed: cleanup returns Promise, dist/index.js existence check added, assertContentOrder handles same-message case, stream.return uses valid void cast.

title: assertContentOrder fails when both substrings appear in the same message
close_reason: Fixed: cleanup returns Promise, dist/index.js existence check added, assertContentOrder handles same-message case, stream.return uses valid void cast.

title: verify-then-sequence asserts 'done' but /echo-back done may echo more than just 'done'
description:
In test/integration/verify-then-sequence.ts line 40, `assertContentPresent(messages, 'done')` checks that the word 'done' appears somewhere in the message history. This is meant to verify that `/echo-back done` was invoked and the model echoed it back.

However, 'done' is a common English word. The LLM could produce 'done' in its octopus fun facts response or summary (e.g., 'When done hunting...') without the echo-back command ever firing. The assertion would pass spuriously.

A more robust check would verify that 'done' appears in a message after the summary, or use a more distinctive argument like 'ECHO_SENTINEL_DONE' instead of the generic word 'done'.

This is a minor robustness concern — in practice the ordering assertion (line 43) provides additional protection.
close_reason: Acknowledged as a minor robustness concern. The ordering assertion provides additional protection against false positives. Deferring to a future improvement since changing the sentinel word requires modifying the fixture spec.

title: then-chaining.ts: readCommandThenChain swallows all filesystem errors silently
close_reason: Now only silently returns [] for ENOENT. Other filesystem errors (permission denied, etc.) are logged as warnings before returning [].

title: Add npm plugin entry point for OpenCode auto-loading
description:
OpenCode loads npm plugins by calling every exported function as fn(pluginInput). Our main index.ts exports factory functions, a class, and an object — none of which work when called that way.

Create src/plugin.ts as a separate entry point that exports pre-instantiated Plugin values (ThenChainingPlugin() and BeadsPlugin() already called). Update package.json main/exports so OpenCode loads this entry point while the library API remains available at a sub-path.
close_reason: Created src/plugin.ts as the npm entry point with pre-instantiated Plugin exports. Package.json main/exports point to plugin.js for OpenCode auto-loading. Library API with factories available at /api sub-path.

title: synthetic-filter.ts: Hand-rolled types instead of SDK Message/Part types
description:
In synthetic-filter.ts, the `isSyntheticMessage` function (line 18) uses a hand-rolled inline type:

```typescript
function isSyntheticMessage(entry: {
  info: { role: string }
  parts: Array<{ type: string; synthetic?: boolean }>
}): boolean
```

This should use the SDK's `Message` and `Part` types from `@opencode-ai/sdk` (or via `@opencode-ai/plugin`). The current approach:

1. Doesn't include `sessionID` in the `info` type, which forces an untyped access on line 60 (`lastMessage.info.sessionID`) — this compiles only because the hook's output type provides the full `Message` type, but the `isSyntheticMessage` function's parameter type doesn't reflect it.

2. Uses `Array<{ type: string; synthetic?: boolean }>` for parts, which doesn't match the actual `Part` union type (TextPart | ToolPart | ReasoningPart | etc.). Only `TextPart` has the `synthetic` field.

Using the SDK types would provide better type safety and catch issues at compile time if the SDK types change.

Affected file: src/then-chaining/synthetic-filter.ts, lines 18-29 and 60.
close_reason: Replaced hand-rolled types with SDK Message and Part types from @opencode-ai/sdk. isSyntheticMessage now takes MessageEntry typed with proper SDK types.

title: No test coverage for then-chaining module
description:
The entire then-chaining module (frontmatter.ts, state.ts, executor.ts, synthetic-filter.ts) and the plugin wiring (plugins/then-chaining.ts) have no unit tests. These are all highly testable components:

1. **frontmatter.ts** — Pure function, easy to test all three syntactic forms, empty/missing values, malformed YAML, non-string array entries.

2. **state.ts** — Pure data structure, easy to test pushChain/advance/popChain/interrupt lifecycle, max depth enforcement, multi-session isolation, cleanup on empty stack.

3. **executor.ts** — Can be tested with a mock client, verifying prompt vs command dispatch, argument splitting, error handling, recursive unwinding, pendingDispatches tracking.

4. **synthetic-filter.ts** — Can be tested with a mock state manager, verifying all three behaviors (keep/remove/replace), synthetic detection, chain-active vs chain-inactive paths.

5. **plugins/then-chaining.ts** — Integration-level tests verifying hook wiring, user interruption vs chain dispatch, file reading, chain lifecycle.

The lack of tests is especially concerning for the executor error handling bug (omg-hbr) and the state manager edge cases. These are the kinds of bugs that tests would catch immediately.

Consider using vitest or bun's built-in test runner.
close_reason: Added comprehensive unit tests for all then-chaining modules: frontmatter.test.ts (17 tests), state.test.ts (19 tests), executor.test.ts (13 tests), synthetic-filter.test.ts (11 tests). 60 tests total, all passing. Added @types/bun, tsconfig.build.json for clean build/test separation, test script in package.json, and relaxed lint rules for test files.

title: ESLint config uses legacy rules spread instead of flat config presets
description:
**File:** eslint.config.js, lines 20-21

The current config spreads rules from the legacy config objects:
```js
rules: {
  ...eslint.configs.recommended.rules,
  ...eslint.configs["recommended-type-checked"].rules,
}
```

While this works (lint passes clean), the canonical approach for ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint v8 is to use the flat config presets directly:
```js
import tseslint from 'typescript-eslint'

export default tseslint.config(
  ...tseslint.configs.recommendedTypeChecked,
  { languageOptions: { parserOptions: { project: './tsconfig.json' } } },
  { ignores: ['dist/', 'node_modules/', '.opencode/'] },
)
```

The `typescript-eslint` package (note: not `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`) provides a `config()` helper and flat config arrays that properly set up the parser, plugin, and rules together. The current approach works but may miss parser/plugin configuration that the flat presets include.

This is a nit — the current config functions correctly. But upgrading to the canonical pattern would be more maintainable and consistent with the typescript-eslint documentation.

**Note:** The `typescript-eslint` unified package is not currently in devDependencies. Adding it would replace both `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` and `@typescript-eslint/parser`.
close_reason: Acknowledged as nit. Current config works correctly. Deferring migration to typescript-eslint unified package to a future iteration.

title: executor.ts: Failed prompt/command dispatch stalls chain instead of skipping
description:
In executor.ts processNext(), when client.session.prompt() or client.session.command() throws an error, the error is caught and logged (lines 74-78 and 111-115), but the method still returns `true`. This tells the caller (chat.message hook) that an entry was dispatched, so it will wait for a model response. But since the prompt/command was never actually sent, no chat.message event will fire, and the chain will stall permanently.

The spec says: 'If client.session.prompt() or client.session.command() throws, log the error and advance to the next entry (skip the failed one).'

The fix is to call `return this.processNext(sessionID)` in the catch block instead of falling through to `return true`, so the chain advances to the next entry after a failure.

Affected lines: executor.ts:74-80 (prompt path) and executor.ts:111-119 (command path).
close_reason: Fixed chain stall bug. On dispatch failure, processNext now recursively advances to the next entry instead of returning true (which would stall the chain waiting for a response that never comes).

title: assertContentPresent in verify-no-then checks user message not assistant response
description:
In test/integration/verify-no-then.ts line 37, `assertContentPresent(messages, 'hello')` is meant to verify the assistant echoed 'hello' back. However, the user message (the command invocation with argument 'hello') also contains 'hello'. The assertion passes even if the assistant response is completely empty or contains an error, because the user message satisfies the check.

Fix: Add an `assertContentPresentInRole(messages, substring, role)` helper or filter messages by role before checking:

```typescript
const assistantMessages = messages.filter(m => m.info.role === 'assistant')
results.push(assertContentPresent(assistantMessages, 'hello'))
```

This is a correctness issue — the test can pass when the feature is broken.
close_reason: Fixed: now filters to assistant-only messages before checking for 'hello' content, preventing false passes from the user command itself.

title: Spec: Integrate existing beads setup
description:
# Integrate Existing Beads Setup

## Overview

Ship the existing beads workflow — currently a local `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts`
plugin plus a collection of commands, agents, skills, and prompts in `.opencode/` —
as part of the `@toady00/open-mardi-gras` npm package. This gives anyone who
installs the package access to the full beads-powered spec → decompose → work → review
workflow without manually copying files.

The integration also requires resolving a known conflict between the BeadsPlugin
and the ThenChainingPlugin. Both plugins hook into `session.idle` and `chat.message`
events, and the beads context injection races with then-chain advancement, causing
interleaved messages and broken chains. This must be fixed before the two plugins
can ship together.

### Why

The beads workflow is currently only usable in this repo because the commands,
agents, skills, and plugin code live in `.opencode/` (local development files).
Packaging them means:

1. Other projects can use the full spec/decompose/work/review workflow by
   installing a single npm package and running a setup command.
2. The two plugins (ThenChaining and Beads) are tested and validated to work
   together without interference.
3. The HelloWorldPlugin placeholder is removed.

### What Changes

- New `BeadsPlugin` export in `src/plugins/beads.ts`
- New `omg-build` agent shipped with the package
- New CLI setup command: `npx @toady00/open-mardi-gras setup`
- All shipped commands/agents/skills renamed from `bd-` prefix to `omg-` prefix
- HelloWorldPlugin removed
- Local `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` converted to a thin import wrapper
- Plugin conflict between BeadsPlugin and ThenChainingPlugin resolved

---

## Requirements

### R1: BeadsPlugin runtime (`src/plugins/beads.ts`)

Port the existing `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` into `src/plugins/beads.ts` as an
exported plugin factory function, matching the pattern of `ThenChainingPlugin`.

**R1.1** Export `BeadsPlugin` as a named export from `src/index.ts`.

**R1.2** On first user message per session (`chat.message` hook), run `bd sync`
then `bd prime` and inject the output as a synthetic user message wrapped in
`<beads-context>` tags, followed by a `<beads-guidance>` block. If `bd prime`
returns empty output, skip injection silently.

**R1.3** On `session.compacted` event, re-inject beads context (same as R1.2).

**R1.4** On `session.idle` event, run `bd sync` quietly (no context injection).

**R1.5** Skip injection if beads context already exists in the session
(handles plugin reload/reconnection). Check by scanning recent messages for
the `<beads-context>` tag.

**R1.6** The plugin must accept zero configuration for now. The factory function
signature should be `BeadsPlugin(): Plugin` (no config parameter yet).

**R1.7** All `bd` and shell invocations must be wrapped in try/catch. On error,
log via `client.app.log` and continue. The plugin must never crash OpenCode.

### R2: Plugin conflict resolution (BeadsPlugin × ThenChainingPlugin)

The BeadsPlugin and ThenChainingPlugin both subscribe to `session.idle` and
`chat.message`. When both are active, the beads context injection races with
then-chain advancement, causing:

- Beads context injected mid-chain as a visible user message, causing the model
  to respond to beads state instead of advancing the chain
- Then-chain steps interleaved with beads responses
- Chain commands failing silently (observed: `undefined is not an object
  (evaluating 'command2.agent')` when dispatching chained commands during
  concurrent plugin activity)

**R2.1** The two plugins must coordinate so that beads context injection
(`bd prime` via `client.session.prompt`) never fires while a then-chain is
actively executing. "Actively executing" means from the moment a command with
a `then` key starts until the chain fully completes or is interrupted.

**R2.2** The beads `session.idle` sync (`bd sync`) is safe to run concurrently
with then-chains because it produces no visible output. This must remain
unchanged — `bd sync` always runs on idle regardless of chain state.

**R2.3** The beads `chat.message` injection (first message per session) fires
before any command has been executed and is guarded by the `injectedSessions`
set (R1.5), so it cannot conflict with then-chains on subsequent messages.

**R2.4** The beads `session.compacted` re-injection must also be deferred if
a then-chain is active. The context should be injected once the chain completes
or is interrupted.

**R2.5** Implementation: introduce a `PluginCoordinator` singleton in
`src/coordination.ts` that both plugins import. The coordinator exposes:

```typescript
// src/coordination.ts
class PluginCoordinator {
  // Called by ThenChainingPlugin to register its ChainStateManager
  registerChainState(manager: ChainStateManager): void

  // Called by BeadsPlugin before injecting context.
  // Returns true if any registered ChainStateManager reports an active
  // chain for the given sessionID.
  isChainActive(sessionID: string): boolean

  // Called by BeadsPlugin to queue a callback for when a chain completes
  // on the given session. The callback fires once, then is removed.
  onChainComplete(sessionID: string, callback: () => void): void

  // Called by ThenChainingPlugin (from ChainExecutor.processNext)
  // when a chain fully completes. Fires and removes all queued callbacks
  // for that session.
  notifyChainComplete(sessionID: string): void
}

// Module-level singleton — both plugins import the same instance
export const coordinator: PluginCoordinator
```

**R2.6** If a then-chain is active when compaction occurs, the beads plugin
must call `coordinator.onChainComplete(sessionID, callback)` to queue the
re-injection. The callback fires when `notifyChainComplete` is called.

**R2.7** The ThenChainingPlugin must call `coordinator.notifyChainComplete()`
from the `ChainExecutor.processNext()` method when it returns `false`
(chain fully complete), and from `ChainStateManager.interrupt()` when a
chain is interrupted by the user. `notifyChainComplete()` must be a no-op
when no callbacks are queued (safe to call unconditionally).

**R2.8** If multiple compactions occur while a chain is active, only one
re-injection should fire when the chain completes (deduplicate queued
callbacks per session).

### R3: CLI setup command

Create a CLI entry point that copies commands, agents, skills, and prompts
into the user's `.opencode/` directory.

**R3.1** Invocation: `npx @toady00/open-mardi-gras setup`

**R3.2** The setup command copies the following files from the package into
the user's project `.opencode/` directory:

| `opencode/commands/omg-spec.md`              | `.opencode/commands/omg-spec.md`          |
| `opencode/commands/omg-spec-track.md`        | `.opencode/commands/omg-spec-track.md`    |
| `opencode/commands/omg-spec-refine.md`       | `.opencode/commands/omg-spec-refine.md`   |
| `opencode/commands/omg-decompose.md`         | `.opencode/commands/omg-decompose.md`     |
| `opencode/commands/omg-status.md`            | `.opencode/commands/omg-status.md`        |

| `opencode/agents/omg-spec-writer.md`         | `.opencode/agents/omg-spec-writer.md`     |
| `opencode/agents/omg-reviewer.md`            | `.opencode/agents/omg-reviewer.md`        |

| `opencode/prompts/omg-workflow.md`           | `.opencode/prompts/omg-workflow.md`       |

**R3.3** The setup command creates any missing directories (`.opencode/commands/`,
`.opencode/agents/`, `.opencode/skills/omg-commands/`, etc.).

**R3.4** If a destination file already exists, overwrite it without prompting.
This supports upgrading to newer versions of the package.

**R3.5** The setup command writes `.workflow.yaml` with the following content:

```yaml

  directory: docs/specs
```

If `.workflow.yaml` already exists, overwrite it.

**R3.6** The setup command prints a summary of what was copied, the package
version, and next steps (e.g., "Add BeadsPlugin() to your opencode config").

**R3.7** Add a `bin` entry to `package.json` with the key
`@toady00/open-mardi-gras` pointing to the setup script (`dist/cli/setup.js`).
Users invoke the setup command as `npx @toady00/open-mardi-gras setup`.

**R3.8** The setup script is written in TypeScript in `src/cli/setup.ts` and
compiled to `dist/cli/setup.js` by the existing build process. The compiled
file must include a `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang. Since `tsc` does not emit
shebangs, add a post-build step to the `build` script in `package.json` that
prepends the shebang to `dist/cli/setup.js` (e.g.,
`echo '#!/usr/bin/env node' | cat - dist/cli/setup.js > tmp && mv tmp dist/cli/setup.js && chmod +x dist/cli/setup.js`).

**R3.9** If the user runs the CLI with no arguments or an unrecognized
argument, print a brief usage message showing available commands (currently
only `setup`) and exit with code 1.

### R4: Shipped file content (rename and update references)

All shipped files must be renamed from the `bd-` prefix to `omg-` and all

  (ThenChainingPlugin has no external dependencies)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### Plugin Runtime

- [ ] `import { BeadsPlugin } from '@toady00/open-mardi-gras'` resolves without error
- [ ] `BeadsPlugin()` returns a valid Plugin that OpenCode can load
- [ ] First user message in a session triggers `bd prime` injection with
      `<beads-context>` tags
- [ ] Subsequent messages in the same session do not re-inject
- [ ] `session.compacted` event triggers re-injection
- [ ] `session.idle` event triggers `bd sync` quietly (no visible message)
- [ ] If `bd prime` returns empty output, no injection occurs

### Plugin Conflict Resolution

- [ ] Running a command with a `then` chain while BeadsPlugin is active
      completes the full chain without beads context being injected mid-chain
- [ ] The beads `bd sync` on `session.idle` still runs during then-chains
      (it's safe — no visible output)
- [ ] After a then-chain completes, beads context injection resumes normally
- [ ] If compaction occurs during a then-chain, beads re-injection is deferred
      until the chain completes, then fires
- [ ] A user-interrupted chain allows beads injection to resume immediately
- [ ] The test-then-command → test-then-prompt chain (the exact scenario from
      the conflict export) completes without beads context interleaving
- [ ] The `PluginCoordinator` works when only ThenChainingPlugin is loaded
- [ ] The `PluginCoordinator` works when only BeadsPlugin is loaded
- [ ] Multiple compactions during a single chain result in only one
      beads re-injection

### CLI Setup

- [ ] The setup command copies all 14 files to the correct `.opencode/`
      subdirectories
- [ ] Missing directories are created automatically
- [ ] Existing files are overwritten without prompting
- [ ] `.workflow.yaml` is written with `specs.directory: docs/specs`
- [ ] Summary of actions and package version is printed to stdout
- [ ] `npm pack` produces a tarball containing the `opencode/` directory
      with all 14 files
- [ ] Running the CLI with no arguments prints usage and exits with code 1

### File Content

- [ ] No shipped file contains the string `bd-` as a command/agent/skill
      prefix (only as CLI commands like `bd create`)
- [ ] No shipped file references `adr-workflow`, `bd-v2-`, or any V2 content
- [ ] All `agent:` frontmatter values reference `omg-` prefixed agents
- [ ] All `/` command references use `omg-` prefix
- [ ] All skill name references use `omg-` prefix
- [ ] All `{file:}` prompt includes reference `omg-workflow.md`
- [ ] Skill SKILL.md frontmatter `name:` fields match their directory names
- [ ] `omg-spec-track.md` has `agent: omg-spec-writer` in frontmatter
- [ ] `omg-work.md` references `agent: omg-build`

### New Agent

- [ ] `omg-build.md` exists in `opencode/agents/`
- [ ] `omg-build.md` includes `{file:../prompts/omg-workflow.md}`
- [ ] `omg-build.md` has full tool access and bash permissions

### Removals

- [ ] `HelloWorldPlugin` is no longer exported
- [ ] `src/plugins/hello-world.ts` does not exist

### Local Development

- [ ] `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` imports from `../../dist/index.js`
- [ ] The local beads plugin still works after `bun run build`
- [ ] Local `.opencode/` files still use `bd-` prefix (unchanged)

### Build & Quality

- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds
- [ ] `bun run lint` passes
- [ ] `bun test` passes
- [ ] README documents BeadsPlugin prerequisites and setup command
- [ ] `dist/cli/setup.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`

---

## Edge Cases

### Plugin coordination

- **Chain starts, compaction happens, chain ends**: The queued beads
  re-injection must fire after the chain completes. If another compaction
  happens before the queued injection fires, only one injection should occur
  (not two).

- **Multiple concurrent sessions**: The coordination state must be per-session.
  A then-chain in session A must not block beads injection in session B.

- **Plugin load order**: The coordination mechanism must work regardless of
  which plugin initializes first. Both plugins import from a shared module,
  so load order is irrelevant.

- **Only one plugin active**: If a user only uses `ThenChainingPlugin` without
  `BeadsPlugin` (or vice versa), each plugin must work independently without
  errors. The shared coordination module must handle the case where only one
  side is present.

### Setup command

- **Existing `.opencode/` with other files**: The setup command only writes
  specific files. It must not delete or modify any files it doesn't own.

---

## Deferred to Follow-Up Spec

The following items are explicitly deferred to a separate "hardening" spec
to be created after this integration is working:

- **Dependency detection**: Lazy PATH checks for `bd` and `yq`, warning
  on missing dependencies, disabling plugin when dependencies are absent
- **Non-interactive setup**: `--yes`/`--no-workflow` flags, TTY detection,
  CI-friendly defaults
- **Version mismatch detection**: Checking that plugin runtime version
  matches the version of setup files installed in `.opencode/`
- **Graceful degradation**: `bd` disappearing mid-session, transient errors,
  retry logic
- **Per-session vs. global disabling**: Whether dependency failures disable
  the plugin globally or per-session

---

## Out of Scope

- V2 commands, agents, and skills (ADR-aware variants)
- ADR workflow (commands, agents, `adr-workflow` skill)
- Plugin configuration options (future: selective feature activation)
- Programmatic command/agent registration via the OpenCode `config` hook
  (may be explored in the future if OpenCode supports it)
- Conditional/parallel then-chaining
- Automatic `bd init` or beads project setup

---

## Open Questions

None — all questions resolved during spec refinement.

title: PluginCoordinator singleton (src/coordination.ts)
description:
## What

Create `src/coordination.ts` exporting a `PluginCoordinator` class and a module-level singleton `coordinator`.

## Why

Both BeadsPlugin and ThenChainingPlugin need to coordinate around session events. The coordinator is the shared dependency that both plugins import. It must exist before either plugin can reference it.

## Implementation

Create `src/coordination.ts` with the following API:

```typescript
// Interface for what the coordinator needs from a chain state manager.
// Avoids importing the concrete ChainStateManager class, keeping the
// coordination module decoupled from then-chaining internals.
export interface ChainStateProvider {
  hasActiveChain(sessionID: string): boolean
}

export class PluginCoordinator {
  registerChainState(provider: ChainStateProvider): void
  isChainActive(sessionID: string): boolean
  onChainComplete(sessionID: string, callback: () => void): void
  notifyChainComplete(sessionID: string): void
}

export const coordinator: PluginCoordinator
```

### Key behaviors (spec R2.5–R2.8):

- `registerChainState(provider)`: Stores a reference to the provider. Called by ThenChainingPlugin during init. The `ChainStateManager` class already satisfies the `ChainStateProvider` interface (it has `hasActiveChain(sessionID)`).
- `isChainActive(sessionID)`: Queries all registered providers to check if any chain is active for the given session. Returns `false` if no providers are registered (safe when only BeadsPlugin is loaded).
- `onChainComplete(sessionID, callback)`: Queues a callback to fire when `notifyChainComplete` is called for that session. **Deduplicates**: if a callback is already queued for a session, replace it (don't stack). This handles R2.8 (multiple compactions during a chain → only one re-injection).
- `notifyChainComplete(sessionID)`: Fires and removes all queued callbacks for the session. Must be a no-op when no callbacks are queued (safe to call unconditionally).

### Internal state:
- `private providers: ChainStateProvider[]` — registered chain state providers
- `private pendingCallbacks: Map<string, () => void>` — one callback per session (deduplication)

### Coordination state is per-session:
A then-chain in session A must not block beads injection in session B. The `isChainActive()` and callback maps are keyed by sessionID.

### Export from `src/index.ts`:
Add: `export { coordinator, PluginCoordinator } from './coordination.js'`
Also export the `ChainStateProvider` type.

## Where

- New file: `src/coordination.ts`
- Modified: `src/index.ts` (add exports)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `coordinator` is a module-level singleton (both plugins get the same instance)
- [ ] `ChainStateProvider` interface is exported (not the concrete ChainStateManager)
- [ ] `isChainActive()` returns `false` when no providers are registered
- [ ] `onChainComplete()` deduplicates callbacks per session (R2.8)
- [ ] `notifyChainComplete()` is a no-op when no callbacks are queued
- [ ] Per-session isolation: chain in session A doesn't affect session B
- [ ] Unit tests in `src/coordination.test.ts` cover all methods and edge cases
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds
close_reason: Created src/coordination.ts with PluginCoordinator class and module-level singleton. Exports coordinator, PluginCoordinator, and ChainStateProvider from index. All 10 unit tests pass covering: no-provider fallback, multi-provider queries, callback dedup, per-session isolation, no-op notify. Build succeeds.

title: No unit tests for BeadsPlugin
description:
## Location

`src/plugins/beads.ts` — no corresponding test file exists

## Problem

The BeadsPlugin has no unit tests. The PluginCoordinator has thorough tests (`src/coordination.test.ts`, 10 tests), and ThenChainingPlugin has extensive tests across multiple files. But the BeadsPlugin — which has non-trivial logic including:

1. First-message-per-session guard (`injectedSessions` Set)
2. Existing injection detection (scanning message parts for `<beads-context>`)
3. Coordinator integration (deferred injection during active chains)
4. Error handling in multiple code paths

— has zero test coverage.

## Impact

Regressions in the injection logic, coordinator integration, or error handling paths would not be caught by the test suite.

## Suggested Tests

- Factory function returns a valid Plugin shape
- First message triggers injection, second message skips
- Existing `<beads-context>` in messages prevents re-injection
- `session.compacted` triggers re-injection
- `session.compacted` defers when chain is active
- `session.idle` runs `bd sync` quietly
- Empty `bd prime` output skips injection
- `bd` command failures are caught and logged, not thrown
close_reason: Created src/plugins/beads.test.ts with 9 tests covering: factory function shape, hook initialization, first-message injection, skip on second message, skip on existing beads context, skip on empty prime output, session.idle sync, session.compacted re-injection, and error handling for bd failures. Relaxed lint rules for test files. All 83 tests pass.

```
echo '#!/usr/bin/env node' | cat - dist/cli/setup.js > tmp && mv tmp dist/cli/setup.js && chmod +x dist/cli/setup.js
```

## Problem

The build script writes to a file called `tmp` in the project root. This has two minor issues:

1. If the build is interrupted between `> tmp` and `mv tmp`, a stale `tmp` file is left in the project root.
2. The name `tmp` could collide with an existing file (unlikely but possible).

## Suggested Fix

Use `mktemp` or write to `dist/cli/setup.js.tmp` instead:

```
echo '#!/usr/bin/env node' | cat - dist/cli/setup.js > dist/cli/setup.js.tmp && mv dist/cli/setup.js.tmp dist/cli/setup.js && chmod +x dist/cli/setup.js
```

Also consider adding `tmp` to `.gitignore` as a safety measure.
close_reason: Changed build script to use dist/cli/setup.js.tmp instead of tmp in project root.

title: Setup command has no error handling for file copy failures
description:
## Location

`src/cli/setup.ts` lines 33-41

## Problem

The setup command's file copy loop has no try/catch:

```typescript
for (const file of FILES_TO_COPY) {
  const src = join(sourceRoot, file)
  const dest = join(destRoot, file)
  mkdirSync(dirname(dest), { recursive: true })
  copyFileSync(src, dest)
  console.log(\`  copied: .opencode/\${file}\`)
  copied++
}
```

If any file fails to copy (e.g., permission denied on the destination, source file missing from a corrupted install), the command crashes with an unhandled exception and a raw Node.js stack trace. The user gets no indication of which files were successfully copied and which weren't.

## Impact

Poor user experience on setup failure. Partial setup state with no clear recovery path.

## Fix

Wrap the loop body in try/catch, log which file failed, and either continue (best-effort) or exit with a clear error message and non-zero exit code.
close_reason: Added try/catch around file copy loop in setup command. Individual failures are logged with the file path and error message. After all files are attempted, a summary shows failed count and exits with code 1 if any failed.

title: Duplicate logger helper in BeadsPlugin and ThenChainingPlugin
close_reason: Extracted createPluginLogger to src/logging.ts, updated both plugins to use it.

title: session.compacted handler does not clear injectedSessions for the session
description:
## Location

`src/plugins/beads.ts` lines 167-186

## Problem

When `session.compacted` fires, the handler re-injects beads context but does NOT clear the session from `injectedSessions`. This is actually correct for the current flow (compaction re-injects directly), but there's a subtle issue:

After compaction, the `chat.message` handler will still see the session in `injectedSessions` and skip injection. This is fine because compaction already re-injected. However, if the compacted re-injection was deferred (chain active) and then the chain completes but the deferred callback fails silently, the session would have stale context with no way to re-inject on the next message.

This is a minor robustness concern, not a bug in the happy path. The current behavior is correct for normal operation.

## Impact

Very low — only affects the edge case where deferred injection fails AND the user continues the session. The session would have no beads context but the plugin would think it was already injected.

## Suggested Fix

Consider clearing the sessionID from `injectedSessions` on compaction so the next `chat.message` can serve as a fallback injection point if the compaction injection fails.
close_reason: Added injectedSessions.delete(sessionID) at start of session.compacted handler so chat.message can serve as fallback injection.

title: Multiple eslint-disable comments suppress type safety in BeadsPlugin
description:
## Location

`src/plugins/beads.ts` lines 73-77, 140-144

## Problem

The BeadsPlugin has 6 eslint-disable comments suppressing TypeScript safety rules:

1. Lines 73-74: `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-call`, `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-member-access` for \`bd sync\`.quiet()
2. Lines 76-77: Same rules plus `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment` for \`bd prime\`.text()
3. Lines 140-141: `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment`, `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any`, `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-member-access` for message parts access
4. Lines 143-144: `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-call`, `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-member-access` for parts.some()

The shell template literal (\`$\`) type issues (items 1-2) are likely due to the `@opencode-ai/plugin` shell type not being fully typed. The message parts access (items 3-4) is due to the OpenCode SDK not exposing `parts` in the message type.

## Impact

These suppressions are pragmatic given the SDK's type limitations, but they hide potential runtime errors. The `(msg as any).parts || (msg.info as any).parts` pattern on line 141 is particularly fragile — if the SDK changes its message shape, this breaks silently.

## Suggested Fix

Consider adding a typed helper function with a single `@ts-expect-error` or `as unknown as` cast at the boundary, rather than scattering inline suppressions. This would centralize the type-unsafe code.
close_reason: Accepted: eslint-disable comments are necessary due to @opencode-ai/plugin SDK type limitations on shell template tag and message parts. These are at the SDK boundary and can be removed when the SDK improves its types.

title: PluginCoordinator singleton has no reset method for testing
description:
## Location

`src/coordination.ts` line 63

## Problem

The module-level singleton `export const coordinator = new PluginCoordinator()` accumulates state across test runs. The test file (`src/coordination.test.ts`) works around this by creating fresh `new PluginCoordinator()` instances in each test, which is correct. However, integration tests that import the singleton directly would share state between tests.

## Impact

Very low — the current test approach (fresh instances) is fine. This only matters if future integration tests use the singleton directly.

## Suggested Fix

Consider adding a `reset()` method to `PluginCoordinator` that clears providers and pending callbacks, or export a `createCoordinator()` factory alongside the singleton.
close_reason: Accepted: Tests already create fresh PluginCoordinator instances per test. A reset method would only be needed for future integration tests that import the singleton directly. Low priority.

title: BeadsPlugin runtime (src/plugins/beads.ts)
description:
## What

Port the existing `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` into `src/plugins/beads.ts` as an exported plugin factory function, matching the pattern of `ThenChainingPlugin`.

## Why

The beads plugin currently lives in `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` as a local development file. It needs to be a proper package export so consumers can `import { BeadsPlugin } from '@toady00/open-mardi-gras'`.

## Implementation

### Factory function pattern (spec R1.6):
The current plugin is exported as `const BeadsPlugin: Plugin = async ({ client, $ }) => { ... }`. Convert it to a factory function: `export function BeadsPlugin(): Plugin` that returns the async plugin function. This matches ThenChainingPlugin's pattern (see `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`).

### Event hooks (spec R1.1–R1.5):

1. **`chat.message`** (R1.2, R1.5): On first user message per session, run `bd sync` then `bd prime`, inject output as synthetic user message wrapped in `<beads-context>` tags followed by `<beads-guidance>` block. Skip if `bd prime` returns empty. Skip if beads context already exists in session (scan recent messages for `<beads-context>` tag).

2. **`session.compacted`** (R1.3): Re-inject beads context. **Must coordinate with PluginCoordinator** — if a then-chain is active, defer injection via `coordinator.onChainComplete()` (R2.4, R2.6).

3. **`session.idle`** (R1.4): Run `bd sync` quietly. This is always safe regardless of chain state (R2.2) — no coordination needed.

### Coordinator integration (spec R2.1–R2.4, R2.6):
- Import `coordinator` from `../coordination.js`
- Before injecting context on `session.compacted`, check `coordinator.isChainActive(sessionID)`
- If chain is active, call `coordinator.onChainComplete(sessionID, () => injectBeadsContext(...))`
- The `chat.message` injection fires before any command has been executed and is guarded by `injectedSessions`, so it cannot conflict with then-chains (R2.3)

### Error handling (spec R1.7):
All `bd` and shell invocations must be wrapped in try/catch. On error, log via `client.app.log` and continue. The plugin must never crash OpenCode.

### Port the helper functions:
- `getSessionContext()` — queries session messages for model/agent context
- `injectBeadsContext()` — runs bd sync + bd prime, injects wrapped output

### Export from `src/index.ts` (R1.1):
Add `export { BeadsPlugin } from './plugins/beads.js'`

## Where

- New file: `src/plugins/beads.ts`
- Modified: `src/index.ts` (add export)
- Reference: `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` (existing implementation to port)
- Reference: `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts` (factory function pattern to follow)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `import { BeadsPlugin } from '@toady00/open-mardi-gras'` resolves without error
- [ ] `BeadsPlugin()` returns a valid Plugin that OpenCode can load
- [ ] First user message triggers `bd prime` injection with `<beads-context>` tags
- [ ] Subsequent messages in same session do not re-inject
- [ ] `session.compacted` triggers re-injection (deferred if chain active)
- [ ] `session.idle` triggers `bd sync` quietly (no visible message)
- [ ] Empty `bd prime` output → no injection
- [ ] All bd invocations wrapped in try/catch, errors logged not thrown
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds with the new file
close_reason: Ported BeadsPlugin to src/plugins/beads.ts as factory function matching ThenChainingPlugin pattern. Integrated coordinator for deferred injection during active chains. Added BeadsPlugin export to index. All 73 tests pass, build succeeds.

title: Wire ThenChainingPlugin into PluginCoordinator
description:
## What

Update the ThenChainingPlugin to register its ChainStateManager with the PluginCoordinator and call `notifyChainComplete()` at the right lifecycle points.

## Why

The ThenChainingPlugin must participate in coordination so the BeadsPlugin knows when chains are active and when they complete. Without this, beads context injection can race with chain advancement (the original bug described in spec R2).

## Implementation

### Register ChainStateManager (spec R2.5):
In `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`, after creating the `ChainStateManager` (line 37), call:
```typescript
import { coordinator } from '../coordination.js'
coordinator.registerChainState(stateManager)
```

### Notify on chain completion (spec R2.7):
In `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`, in the `event` handler for `session.idle` (around line 177), after `executor.processNext(sessionID)` returns `false`, call:
```typescript
if (!dispatched) {
  coordinator.notifyChainComplete(sessionID)
  await logger('info', 'Then chain: completed')
}
```

### Notify on chain interruption (spec R2.7):
In `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`, at every call site where `stateManager.interrupt(sessionID)` is called (lines 107 and 138), add `coordinator.notifyChainComplete(sessionID)` immediately after. There are two interrupt sites:
1. Line ~107: user command with no then-chain interrupts active chain
2. Line ~138: user command with a then-chain interrupts and replaces active chain

**Important**: Do NOT modify `src/then-chaining/state.ts` or `src/then-chaining/executor.ts` to add coordinator calls. The coordinator is a plugin-level concern — keep it in the plugin file where the call sites already exist. The state and executor modules should remain coordinator-unaware.

### Safety:
`notifyChainComplete()` is a no-op when no callbacks are queued, so it's safe to call unconditionally. No conditional logic needed.

## Where

- Modified: `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts` (register chain state + notify calls)
- NOT modified: `src/then-chaining/executor.ts`, `src/then-chaining/state.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ChainStateManager is registered with coordinator during plugin init
- [ ] `notifyChainComplete()` called when `processNext()` returns false (chain done)
- [ ] `notifyChainComplete()` called after each `stateManager.interrupt()` call
- [ ] Existing then-chaining tests still pass (`bun test`)
- [ ] ThenChainingPlugin works correctly when BeadsPlugin is NOT loaded (coordinator has no queued callbacks → no-op)
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds
close_reason: Wired ThenChainingPlugin into PluginCoordinator: registered ChainStateManager on init, added notifyChainComplete calls at both interrupt sites and on chain completion. All 73 tests pass. Build succeeds.

title: CLI setup command (src/cli/setup.ts)
description:
## What

Create the CLI entry point at `src/cli/setup.ts` that copies shipped opencode files into the user's project `.opencode/` directory.

## Why

Users need a single command (`npx @toady00/open-mardi-gras setup`) to install all the workflow files (commands, agents, skills, prompts) into their project. This is the primary onboarding mechanism for the beads workflow.

## Implementation

### Entry point (spec R3.1, R3.9):
`src/cli/setup.ts` is the main CLI script. It parses `process.argv`:
- If first arg is `setup`, run the setup logic
- Otherwise (no args or unrecognized arg), print usage message and `process.exit(1)`

### Setup logic (spec R3.2–R3.6):

1. **Resolve paths**: The package's `opencode/` directory is at `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../opencode')` (relative to `dist/cli/setup.js`). The destination is `path.resolve(process.cwd(), '.opencode')`.

2. **Copy files** (R3.2): Copy all 14 files from the package's `opencode/` tree to the project's `.opencode/` tree, preserving subdirectory structure:
   - `opencode/commands/*` → `.opencode/commands/*`
   - `opencode/agents/*` → `.opencode/agents/*`
   - `opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md` → `.opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md`
   - `opencode/skills/omg-epics/SKILL.md` → `.opencode/skills/omg-epics/SKILL.md`
   - `opencode/prompts/*` → `.opencode/prompts/*`

3. **Create directories** (R3.3): Use `fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })` before each copy.

4. **Overwrite existing** (R3.4): Use `fs.copyFileSync()` which overwrites by default.

5. **Write .workflow.yaml** (R3.5): Write to `path.resolve(process.cwd(), '.workflow.yaml')`:
   ```yaml
   specs:
     directory: docs/specs
   ```

6. **Print summary** (R3.6): Print each file copied, the package version (read from `package.json` via `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../package.json')`), and next steps: "Add BeadsPlugin() to your opencode config".

### Edge case (spec Edge Cases → Setup):
The setup command only writes specific files. It must NOT delete or modify any files it doesn't own in `.opencode/`.

### Note:
The `package.json` changes (bin entry, build script shebang step, files array) are handled in the "Package.json updates" task (omg-lkv.8). This task only creates the TypeScript source file.

## Where

- New file: `src/cli/setup.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/cli/setup.ts` compiles without errors
- [ ] Setup logic copies all 14 files to correct `.opencode/` subdirectories
- [ ] Missing directories are created automatically
- [ ] Existing files are overwritten without prompting
- [ ] `.workflow.yaml` is written with `specs.directory: docs/specs`
- [ ] Summary of actions and package version printed to stdout
- [ ] Running CLI with no arguments prints usage and exits with code 1
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds
close_reason: Created src/cli/setup.ts with setup command that copies all 14 opencode files to .opencode/, writes .workflow.yaml, prints summary with version. Usage handler exits with code 1 on invalid args. Build succeeds with shebang prepended to dist/cli/setup.js.

title: Update local dev wrapper (.opencode/plugins/beads.ts) (R8)
description:
## What

Convert `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` from a full plugin implementation to a thin import wrapper that imports `BeadsPlugin` from the compiled dist output.

## Why

The full plugin implementation is moving to `src/plugins/beads.ts` (the package export). The local `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` should become a thin wrapper — just like `.opencode/plugins/then-chaining.ts` already is — so that local development uses the same code path as consumers.

## Implementation

Replace the entire contents of `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` with:

```typescript
/**
 * Local development wrapper for the BeadsPlugin.
 *
 * Imports from the compiled dist/ output.
 * Run `bun run build` before restarting OpenCode to pick up changes.
 */

import type { Plugin } from '@opencode-ai/plugin'
import { BeadsPlugin } from '../../dist/index.js'

export const BeadsPluginLocal: Plugin = BeadsPlugin()
```

This matches the exact pattern of `.opencode/plugins/then-chaining.ts`.

### Important (spec R8.2):
The local `.opencode/` commands, agents, skills, and prompts continue to use the `bd-` prefix. Only the shipped copies in `opencode/` use `omg-`. Do NOT rename any local `.opencode/` files.

## Where

- Modified: `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` imports `BeadsPlugin` from `../../dist/index.js`
- [ ] The wrapper follows the same pattern as `.opencode/plugins/then-chaining.ts`
- [ ] The local beads plugin still works after `bun run build`
- [ ] Local `.opencode/` files still use `bd-` prefix (unchanged)
close_reason: Replaced full BeadsPlugin implementation in .opencode/plugins/beads.ts with thin import wrapper matching the then-chaining.ts pattern. Imports BeadsPlugin from dist/index.js. Local .opencode/ files unchanged (still bd- prefix).

title: Package.json updates and README documentation (R9)
description:
## What

Update `package.json` with the `bin` entry, `files` array addition, build script shebang step, and update the README with BeadsPlugin documentation.

## Why

The package needs to ship the `opencode/` directory, expose the CLI setup command, and ensure the CLI has a proper shebang. The README must document the new plugin and its prerequisites so users know how to set it up.

## Implementation

### package.json changes:

1. **bin entry** (spec R9.1, R3.7): Add:
   ```json
   "bin": {
     "@toady00/open-mardi-gras": "dist/cli/setup.js"
   }
   ```

2. **files array** (spec R9.2): Add `"opencode/"` to the existing `files` array:
   ```json
   "files": [
     "dist/",
     "opencode/",
     "LICENSE",
     "README.md",
     "CHANGELOG.md"
   ]
   ```

3. **build script shebang** (spec R3.8): Update the `build` script to prepend `#!/usr/bin/env node` to `dist/cli/setup.js` after compilation:
   ```json
   "build": "tsc -p tsconfig.build.json && echo '#!/usr/bin/env node' | cat - dist/cli/setup.js > tmp && mv tmp dist/cli/setup.js && chmod +x dist/cli/setup.js"
   ```
   This is needed because `tsc` does not emit shebangs.

### README updates (spec R9.3):

Add documentation sections for:

1. **BeadsPlugin usage**: Show how to add `BeadsPlugin()` to an opencode config alongside `ThenChainingPlugin()`. Example:
   ```typescript
   import { ThenChainingPlugin, BeadsPlugin } from '@toady00/open-mardi-gras'
   export default {
     plugins: [ThenChainingPlugin(), BeadsPlugin()]
   }
   ```

2. **Prerequisites**: Document that `bd` (beads CLI) and `yq` must be on `$PATH` for BeadsPlugin to work. Note that these are only required if using BeadsPlugin — ThenChainingPlugin has no external dependencies.

3. **Setup command**: Document `npx @toady00/open-mardi-gras setup` and what it does (copies workflow files to `.opencode/`, writes `.workflow.yaml`).

title: Build verification and quality gates

## What

Run all quality gates to verify the complete integration works end-to-end: build, lint, test, and verify the npm package contents.

## Why

This is the final verification step before code review. All the pieces (coordinator, plugins, shipped files, CLI, package.json) must work together. A broken build or failing tests would block the review.

## Implementation

### Quality gates to run:

1. **Build**: `bun run build` must succeed
2. **Lint**: `bun run lint` must pass
3. **Tests**: `bun test` must pass (existing tests + any new coordinator tests)
4. **Shebang check**: Verify `dist/cli/setup.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`
5. **Package contents**: Run `npm pack --dry-run` and verify:
   - `opencode/` directory with all 14 files is included
   - `dist/cli/setup.js` is included
   - `dist/plugins/beads.js` is included
   - `dist/coordination.js` is included
6. **Import check**: Verify `import { BeadsPlugin, ThenChainingPlugin, coordinator } from '@toady00/open-mardi-gras'` resolves

### Fix any issues found:
If any gate fails, fix the issue in the relevant file and re-run. Common issues:
- TypeScript compilation errors from new files
- ESLint violations in new code
- Missing exports in `src/index.ts`
- Shebang not prepended (build script issue)

## Where

No new files — this task runs verification commands and fixes issues across the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bun test` passes
- [ ] `dist/cli/setup.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`
